matches: Four-on-the-floor kick: 'fourfloor' pattern fires a kick on every beat (steps 1,5,9,13) with no variation — the non-negotiable 909 skeleton is correct.
Offbeat open hat: a dedicated openhat voice (longer 0.32s noise decay) on every '+' (the 8th offbeat) matches the signature pumping push-pull. Closed hat on each beat.
Clap/backbeat on 2 & 4: snare-as-clap fires on beat==1 and beat==3 (steps 5,13), correct backbeat placement.
Tempo: defaultBpm 128 sits exactly on the DNA's 'safe typical' of 128 for the combined Detroit/minimal target.
Mode: defaultMode 'aeolian' (natural minor) is the correct core scale.

Sidechain pump exists: algo.sidechain=true triggers triggerSidechain on every kick, ducking pad (floor ~0.32) and bass (floor ~0.74) with a ramp back — the audible 'pump' is implemented as a real ducking envelope, not just mixing.

Near-static harmony: padProg is effectively a 2-chord vamp (i for 8 bars, then a bVI/-2 shape for 8 bars) held over long spans via padStride 4 — matches the 'hypnotic loop, harmony barely moves' trait.
Bass voicing direction is plausible: alkali (MS-20-style fatsawtooth + 24dB LP + distortion), low cutoff 0.35, short decay 0.2 — a punchy gated mono sub is the right ballpark.

gaps.dnaWants: Loop-and-evolve DJ structure: 16-32 bar additive intro (kick+hats first, then sub, then stabs, then pad), at least one BREAKDOWN that drops the kick and exposes pads/stabs with rising filter/reverb, then a re-entry 'drop' (kick+bass return with sidechain pump). Subtle evolution every 8-16 bars via parameter automation. This is the single most load-bearing trait — a literal static loop 'sounds like a demo, not a track.'
gaps.currentImpl: buildOutrunForm over a fixed 16 bars: bass on every bar 0-15, two short lead phrases at bars 4 and 10, pad across the whole form. drumStartBar 0 / drumEndBar 16 with drumIntenseFrom 999, drumBreakFrom 999, drumBreakTo 0 — i.e. the break/intensity logic is permanently disabled. No additive layering, no kick-drop breakdown, no re-entry drop, no per-bar/per-loop filter or send automation anywhere in the engine.
gaps.fix: Build a buildTechnoForm (or extend the form/drum config) that (a) intros with kick+hats only for bars 0-3, adds bass at 4, stabs at 8, pad at 12; (b) sets drumBreakFrom=8,drumBreakTo=10 so the fourfloor branch's isBreak path actually fires (note: the fourfloor branch currently ignores isBreak entirely — add `if(isBreak) skip kick` there); (c) on break, ramp a filter-cutoff up and reverb send up, then re-enter kick+bass at bar 10. Also add the missing capability: a slow per-bar automation lane that ramps pad/stab filter cutoff across the loop (Tone .rampTo on the filter frequency) — without it the loop is static. See needsLayerC.

gaps.dnaWants: The Detroit 'space chord' STAB: a SHORT, punchy minor7/min9 chord stab with a tight pluck envelope, placed on OFFBEATS, soaked in tempo-synced (dotted-8th) delay + reverb, ideally a detuned 2-3 osc saw into a resonant lowpass. This is one of the four identityMustHaves and the thing that most signals 'this is techno.'
gaps.currentImpl: There is no stab voice at all. The pad is a slow vapor (Juno) wash (attack 0.6, release 1.5, padStride 4, sustain 0.5) playing sustained block chords on the downbeat of every 4th bar. The lead is generateMelody with MELODY_PROFILES.noir — a sparse single-note melodic motif, not a rhythmic chord stab. Nothing plays a short off-beat chord stab.
gaps.fix: Add a dedicated 'stab' lead: reuse generateArpLead or a new generator that emits a 3-4 note minor7 chord ([0,2,4,6] in aeolian = root,b3,5,b7) as a single short event placed on offbeats (e.g. the '+' of beats 2 and 4, durations of 1-2 sixteenths), looped every bar. Voice it as detuned saw (alkali or a saw poly) with attack 0.003 / decay 0.12 / sustain 0 / release 0.15 (tight pluck), cutoff ~0.5, resonance ~0.4, send:true into the existing delay (delayTime already 0.5). This is the highest-authenticity-per-effort fix.

gaps.dnaWants: Fixed-interval / 'chord-memory' parallel voicing: define ONE minor-triad/m7 shape and transpose it as a rigid block up/down the scale (e.g. Cm-Gm-Fm-Abm-Bbm), no diatonic re-voicing — plus min7/min9 color (root,b3,5,b7,9), not plain triads.
gaps.currentImpl: padProg cells are diatonic-third stacks [0,2,4] interpreted through buildChordMap + degreeToMidi in AEOLIAN, which produces RE-VOICED diatonic triads (the third changes quality per scale degree). It's a plain minor triad with no 7th/9th color, and the [-2,0,2] second chord is diatonically re-voiced, not a parallel transpose of the same shape.
gaps.fix: Two parts: (1) extend the chord cells to m7 by stacking another third: use [0,2,4,6] for the stab/pad tones so degreeToMidi yields root,b3,5,b7. (2) For the 'chord memory' sound, bypass diatonic re-voicing: pick a fixed semitone shape (minor triad = [0,3,7] in semitones, m7 = [0,3,7,10]) and transpose the whole block by the chord root in SEMITONES rather than mapping each degree through the scale. This needs a small parallel-voicing path in buildChordMap/degree mapping (transpose-as-block), which the DNA flags as 'trivial once recognized.'

gaps.dnaWants: Offbeat sub-bass that sits in the GAPS between kicks (never colliding with the kick fundamental), or a rolling 16th line where offbeats drop an octave; with glide/portamento (SH-101 slide) and accent, heavily sidechained to the kick.
gaps.currentImpl: riffGen = generateRiff(BASS_PROFILES.outrun): grooves like [2,2,2,2,...] starting with a root ON the downbeat (step 0 → degree 0), i.e. the bass hits ON the kick, not in the offbeat gaps. No portamento/glide (alkali model has no portamento; only the unused 'acid' model does). Bass is NOT sidechained in a way that creates the offbeat pocket — and bass duck floor is shallow (~0.74). octaveProb 0.6 gives some octave jumps but placement collides with the kick.
gaps.fix: Give techno its own BASS_PROFILES.techno with an offbeat groove that rests on step 0 (the kick) and places notes on the 'e/+/a' — e.g. groove [ {rest,2}, {root,2} ] repeated, or a rolling 16th line [1]*16 where every odd 16th drops -scaleLen (octave down). Add portamento to the alkali bass voice (or route techno bass through the existing 'acid' mono which already has portamento:0.05). Deepen the bass duck (bassFloor) so the kick clearly pockets the bass.

gaps.dnaWants: Sparse hypnotic motif / repeating 3-6 note cell or arpeggio in a higher octave, often a single 'bleep' tone, with dotted-8th tempo-synced delay creating the rolling Detroit shimmer; pitch transposition over the loop for evolution.
gaps.currentImpl: leadGenA/B = generateMelody with MELODY_PROFILES.noir — a developed melodic motif (grammar A/A/A2/B, contour, ornaments) that plays as two one-off 4-bar phrases at bars 4 and 10. This is a through-composed melody, not a looped hypnotic cell, and it stops (no continuous ostinato). Delay exists (delayTime 0.5 = 1/8, delayWet 0.3) but it is straight 1/8, not dotted-8th, and there's no per-loop pitch transposition for evolution. crystal (DX7 bell) model is a reasonable 'bleep' source.
gaps.fix: Replace the two noir phrases with a single short repeating cell looped for the whole track (like the carpenter arp approach): use generateArpLead or a 3-4 note fixed cell, octave-up, looped every 1-2 bars. Set the lead delay to dotted-8th (delayTime ~0.375 at this tempo, or a 3/16 sync) for the rolling shimmer. For evolution, transpose the cell by the parallel chord root each 8 bars (ties into the fixed-interval harmony fix).

gaps.dnaWants: Open-hat CHOKE (the next closed hat cuts off the ringing open hat), two layered closed-hat timbres alternated, light swing (~52-60%) on hats/perc but NOT the kick, and per-step velocity/accent automation that EVOLVES across the loop. Optional ride doubling the offbeats for the Jeff Mills rolling feel.
gaps.currentImpl: openhat and hat are independent NoiseSynth voices — there is NO choke (the 0.32s open-hat tail rings through the next closed hat instead of being cut). swing is hard-set to 0 for techno (Transport.swing=0), so hats are dead-straight with zero shuffle. Hat velocities are fixed constants (hat 0.28, openhat 0.5) — no accent variation, no evolution. No ride layer, no second closed-hat timbre.
gaps.fix: (1) Choke: on each closed-hat trigger, call openhat.triggerRelease(time) (or ramp openhatVol to -inf) just before the closed hat so the open tail is cut — the cheap fix is to shorten the open-hat decay or release it on the next 8th. (2) Set algo.swing to ~0.12-0.18 (Tone swing maps 0-1; ~55% feel) since Transport.swing on 16n already excludes the kick timing only partially — verify the four-on-floor kick stays straight. (3) Add light accent variation: vary hat vel per 16th (e.g. accent steps 4,8,12,16) and nudge it per-bar for the 'rolling' evolution.

topFixes: Add the Detroit chord STAB voice: short off-beat minor7 chord stab (tight pluck, detuned saw, into delay+reverb) looped every bar. It's missing entirely and is the #1 identity signal of the genre — highest authenticity-per-effort.

Build a real loop-and-evolve arrangement: additive intro (kick+hats -> bass -> stab -> pad), a kick-drop BREAKDOWN, and a re-entry drop with the pump. The break logic is currently hard-disabled (drumBreakFrom 999) and the fourfloor branch ignores isBreak. This is the DNA's single most load-bearing trait.

Add slow parameter automation (filter cutoff / reverb-send ramps over 8-16 bars) so the static loop 'breathes' — the engine has zero automation lanes today, and the DNA calls this 'the single most important capability.'
Fix the bass to sit in the offbeat gaps with glide+deeper sidechain (techno-specific BASS_PROFILES + portamento), instead of the outrun on-downbeat groove that collides with the kick.

matches: Tempo & meter correct: defaultBpm 126 sits dead-center of DNA 122-128, 4/4, swing 0 (straight) — matches 'Straight (no swing)'.
Mode correct: defaultMode 'aeolian' (natural minor) matches the canonical emotional prog-house tonality.
Four-on-the-floor kick is right: the 'fourfloor' drum branch fires a kick on every beat (steps 1,5,9,13) and triggers sidechain on each kick.
Backbeat clap-role is right: snare on beat 2 & 4 (steps 5,13), played at lower velocity as a clap stand-in.
Off-beat open hat is present: openhat on the '+' of every beat (the house 'tss' lift) — a genuine genre signature.

Sidechain pump exists and is wired: sidechain:true + triggerSidechain ducks bass and pad on every kick with an exponential recovery ramp (0.18s bass / 0.32s pad) — the 'breathing' is at least architecturally present.

Pad is a slow-attack detuned-saw (vapor = fatsawtooth count 3, spread 30, attack 0.8s, long release 2.0s) over a static 4-chord aeolian loop with padStride 2 (one chord per ~2 bars) — close to the DNA's emotional breakdown pad and slow harmonic rhythm.
Harmony rhythm is slow/static: padProg holds 4 chords across 16 bars re-read via buildChordMap, matching 'one chord per bar/2 bars, same loop persists'.

gaps.dnaWants: Rolling 16th-note bassline on a fine grid — the defining 3/16+3/16+2/16 cell (notes on steps 1,4,7) or 'offbeat-16ths that dodge the kick steps', root-repetition with octave jumps, short detached decay so it 'rolls', heavy kick-sidechain so it ducks and swells between hits.
gaps.currentImpl: Reuses BASS_PROFILES.outrun whose grooves are 8th/quarter cells ([2,2,2,2,2,2,2,2] = straight 8ths, [3,1,2,2,2,2,4], etc.) on a coarse grid. generateRiff places root/octave on strong steps and tiny moves on weak steps. No 16th rolling cell, no 3+3+2, NO kick-dodging logic — bass notes land ON the kick steps instead of filling the gaps. octaveProb 0.6 over-jumps vs. root-anchored rolling.
gaps.fix: Add a BASS_PROFILES.proghouse with a true 16th grid and the rolling cell, and point riffGen at it. Concretely: grooves like [3,3,2,3,3,2] and [2,2,2,2,2,2,2,2] expressed at the 16th level, with a kick-dodge rule that leaves steps 0,4,8,12 (where the kick lands) empty or ghosted and fills the offbeat 16ths. Keep degree mostly 0 (root) with octaveProb ~0.2 and an occasional +/-scaleLen jump every 4 bars. Shorten bass decay (voicing decay ~0.12, sustain ~0.25, release ~0.1) so notes detach and roll.

gaps.dnaWants: Big bright euphoric SUPERSAW wall-of-saws: 7-9 detuned saw voices, ~12-30 cent unison spread, wide stereo, lush plate/hall reverb, slow filter-env opening on builds, sidechained to pump with the bass. Played as rhythmic chord-locked stabs or a sustained anthemic line.
gaps.currentImpl: lead voicing model is 'crystal' = a DX7-style FM bell (Tone.FMSynth, harmonicity 3, modIndex 8, single sine carrier, detune 0, no unison). This is a thin metallic bell, the opposite of a saw wall. Lead melody comes from generateMelody(dreamwave profile) — a single-note motif line, not chord-locked supersaw stabs. The lead is also NOT in the sidechain (triggerSidechain only ducks padBus and bassBus), so it cannot pump with the drop.
gaps.fix: Switch proghouse lead model to a supersaw. Easiest within the existing engine: use the 'vapor' fatsawtooth path but bump count to 7 and spread to ~25 cents, wide stereo, lead voicing detune ~15, cutoff ~0.7 with fenvAmount up, big reverb send (already send:true; raise reverbWet). Better: add a dedicated 'supersaw' model (Tone.PolySynth(Tone.Synth,{oscillator:{type:'fatsawtooth',count:7,spread:25}})). Then drive the lead from chord-locked stabs (arpExpand/padProg triad at 1/8 or 1/4 in the drop) instead of a dreamwave single-note motif, and route the lead bus through the sidechain duckGain so it pumps.

gaps.dnaWants: Extended additive arrangement phrased in 8/16/32-bar multiples: intro -> rolling-bass groove -> BREAKDOWN (kick drops out, pads swell) -> BUILD-UP (accelerating snare roll 1/8->1/16->1/32 + white-noise upriser + rising filter + beat-skip on last bar) -> DROP (full supersaw + rolling bass + four-on-floor + heavy pump) -> breakdown 2 -> build 2 -> drop -> outro. Energy via gradual layering + filter automation, with a genuine build->drop arc.
gaps.currentImpl: buildOutrunForm produces a flat 16-bar loop: bass every bar (turnaround fill on bar%4==3), two lead phrases at bar 4 and 10, pads + drums across the full 16 bars. drumIntenseFrom/drumBreakFrom = 999 and drumBreakTo = 0, so there is NO breakdown, NO build-up, NO drop. No snare roll, no riser, no filter automation, no layer add/remove, no beat-skip. The genre-defining breakdown->build->drop journey is entirely absent.
gaps.fix: This needs the arrangement/automation engine that does not yet exist (see needsLayerC). Minimum viable: give proghouse its own form builder that (a) holds a pads-only breakdown for bars where kick is muted, (b) runs a build section that schedules an accelerating snare roll (subdivide 1/8->1/16->1/32 across the last 2-4 bars) plus a white-noise riser and a rising lead-filter ramp, (c) drops to full layers with a crash on the downbeat. Use drumBreakFrom/To and drumIntenseFrom (already plumbed) to carve a real breakdown+build, then add a riser/snare-roll FX generator.

gaps.dnaWants: Closed hat on the OFF-beats (steps 3,7,11,15) with velocity variation; open hat sharing the off-beat for the 'tss'; a SHAKER / 16th-note percussion layer for rolling movement; clap HP-filtered ~140 Hz and layered with small track-delay offsets; reverse cymbal/crash on the downbeat of each new 8-bar phrase.
gaps.currentImpl: fourfloor puts the CLOSED hat ON the beat (steps 1,5,9,13) — DNA wants it on the off-beats. Velocity is a flat 0.28 (no variation). There is NO shaker / 16th perc layer, so the rolling 16th motion is missing. Clap is a generic snare voice at vel 0.7 (no ~140 Hz HP, no layered offsets). No reverse cymbal / crash on phrase boundaries.
gaps.fix: In a proghouse-specific drum branch: move the closed hat to the off-beats (beatT + secBeat/2) with velocity variation (~0.22/0.30/0.24/0.27), keep the open-hat 'tss', and add a 16th shaker loop (4 hits per beat at varied low velocity ~0.12-0.20). Filter the clap higher and optionally double it with a ~8ms offset. Add a crash on bar%8==0 beat 0.

gaps.dnaWants: Canonical i-VI-III-VII Aeolian loop (e.g. Am-F-C-G) with m7/add9/sus color, 9th extensions on pads, re-orchestrated over a static loop.
gaps.currentImpl: padProg = [[0,2,4],[-2,0,2],[3,5,7],[-1,1,3]] — plain diatonic triads. Roots are i, then -2 (VI-ish), III, then -1 (VII-ish) which is roughly in the i-VI-III-VII family, but voiced as bare triads with no 7th/9th/sus color. Re-orchestration over the loop does not happen (pad is the only thing carrying the chords; no pad->pluck->stab hand-off).
gaps.fix: Keep the i-VI-III-VII root motion but add chord color: extend pad triads to 4-note voicings ([0,2,4,6] for m7, add a 9th [0,2,4,8]) and let the supersaw lead re-state the same chords as stabs in the drop so the static loop is re-orchestrated rather than just held by one pad.

gaps.dnaWants: Plucky arp (short-decay pluck from the chord triad, arp Up/Down at 1/16, drenched in 1/8-dotted delay + reverb) and build-up FX (white-noise upriser, accelerating snare roll, pitch riser, beat-skip).
gaps.currentImpl: No pluck-arp at all: arp is 'off' for all proghouse tracks and the lead is a single-note FM-bell motif. No build-up FX generators exist (no riser, no snare roll, no pitch sweep). delayTime 0.5 is a straight 1/8, not the 1/8-dotted called for.
gaps.fix: Add a plucky-arp layer: use arpExpand on the padProg triad at 1/16 (mode up/down), short-decay voicing (decay ~0.15, low sustain), routed to a delay set to dotted-1/8 (delayTime ~0.375 of a bar / use Tone '8n.'). Add the build-up FX generators (white-noise NoiseSynth ramp + accelerating hat/snare roll) as part of the build section.

topFixes: Replace the lead voicing: swap the DX7 FM bell ('crystal') for a real supersaw (fatsawtooth count 7, ~25c spread, wide stereo, big reverb) driven as chord-locked stabs and routed through the sidechain — this is the single most identity-defining sound and is currently completely wrong.

Give proghouse a true rolling 16th-note bass (new BASS_PROFILES.proghouse with 3+3+2 cells on a 16th grid + kick-dodging so notes fill the gaps between kicks, short detached decay) instead of the borrowed straight-8th outrun groove — this plus the pump is what 'rolling bass' means.

Build the breakdown->build->drop arrangement: carve a pads-only breakdown, an accelerating snare-roll + white-noise riser + rising filter build, and a full-layer drop with a crash, using the already-plumbed drumBreakFrom/drumIntenseFrom plus a new FX/riser generator (requires the arrangement engine).

Fix the hat placement and add a 16th shaker: move closed hats to the off-beats with velocity variation and layer a 16th-note shaker so the groove has the rolling movement the DNA's canonicalPattern16 specifies.

matches: Tempo + mode correct: defaultBpm 138, defaultMode 'aeolian' (natural minor) lands squarely in the DNA's 136-142 BPM / minor tonal-center spec.
Four-on-the-floor kick is correct: the 'fourfloor' drum branch pushes a kick on every beat (steps 1/5/9/13) and a clap/snare backbeat on beats 2 & 4 (steps 5/13).
Offbeat open-hat is present: openhat is scheduled at beatT + secBeat/2 on every beat = steps 3/7/11/15, exactly the DNA's offbeat-8th open-hat lane.

Sidechain pump is wired and on: algo.sidechain=true, masterState.sidechain=0.8, and triggerSidechain() ducks BOTH the bass bus (floor ~0.76, 0.18s recover) and pad bus (floor ~0.32, 0.32s recover) on every kick — the core trance groove mechanic exists.

Lush wide pad intent is there: pad model 'vapor' (3 detuned fatsawtooth, heavy chorus wet 0.7) with slow attack 1.0s, long release 2.5s, big reverb (size 0.7/wet 0.55), sidechained — a credible detuned-saw string-ensemble pad.

Slow harmonic rhythm is correct: padProg has 4 chords over 16 bars via buildChordMap = 1 chord per 4 bars, and bass/lead read the SAME chordMap, so harmony moves slowly as the DNA wants (1 chord per 1-2 bars or slower).

gaps.dnaWants: Huge wide euphoric SUPERSAW anthem lead — 7+ detuned saw oscillators (JP-8000 lineage), wide unison/stereo, harmonic-minor melody doubled at octave + a 3rd, filter-env opening on the drop, ping-pong delay + big reverb. This is THE identity sound of the genre.
gaps.currentImpl: lead.model = 'crystal' = a Tone.FMSynth DX7-style FM bell (harmonicity 3.0, modIndex 8, sine carrier+modulator). detune 7. Plays generateMelody with MELODY_PROFILES.outrun. No unison saw stack, no octave/3rd doubling, no harmonic minor, no arp. arp:'off'.
gaps.fix: Swap the trance lead to a supersaw voice. Cheapest authentic route in this engine: reuse the 'vapor'/'ether' fatsawtooth path but build a dedicated 'supersaw' model = Tone.MonoSynth/PolySynth oscillator {type:'fatsawtooth', count:7, spread:25}, bright cutoff (~0.8) with a slow filterEnvelope attack (0.15-0.3s) so it 'opens', plus the existing chorus + ping-pong delay (delayWet already 0.45) + reverb. Then set lead.model='supersaw', detune 15-25 cents-equiv, arp stays off for sustained anthem OR add an octave-doubled harmony layer (see harmony gap). Crystal FM bell must go — it is the single biggest timbral miss.

gaps.dnaWants: Rolling 16th offbeat-octave 'gallop': a note on (almost) every 16th, but the 16th that lands ON the kick (steps 1/5/9/13) is OMITTED, offbeat 16ths are played, and the note just before/after each beat jumps UP AN OCTAVE. Mid-forward detuned saw, short decay so each 16th is a punchy tick. Mandatory sidechain (present).
gaps.currentImpl: riffGen = generateRiff(rng, sl, BASS_PROFILES.outrun). Outrun grooves are 16th-ish ([2,2,2,2,2,2,2,2] etc.) but the algorithm places the ROOT on the downbeat (step 0) and octave jumps on STRONG beats (step%4===0) with octaveProb 0.6 — i.e. octave on the beats, root on the offbeats. That is the inverse of the trance rule. Nothing omits the on-kick 16th; bassFill adds a generic turnaround.
gaps.fix: Add a BASS_PROFILES.trance with a dedicated rolling generator (the bass-riff engine already exists). Generate a fixed 16-step cell where step%4===0 (the on-kick 16ths) is a REST/omit, all other 15 offbeat 16ths play duration-1 ticks at the chord root, and the 16th immediately before each beat (steps 4,8,12,16 → indices 3,7,11,15) is pushed +scaleLen (octave up). Wire trance.riffGen to this instead of BASS_PROFILES.outrun, and use buildAcidForm-style per-bar transposition (no bassFill) so the gallop stays relentless. Bass voicing decay is already short (0.15) which is good for the tick.

gaps.dnaWants: Long-form: Intro -> first groove -> beatless BREAKDOWN (beat+bass muted, pads/strings + emerging lead, big reverb) -> BUILD-UP (rising white-noise sweep + accelerating snare/clap roll 8th->16th->32nd + filter open + pitch riser + crash on the '1') -> DROP/anthem (supersaw over rolling bass). The breakdown->build->drop dynamic is what makes it 'feel' like trance.
gaps.currentImpl: buildForm = buildOutrunForm: a flat 16-bar loop, lead at bars 4 and 10, no section staging. trance config sets drumIntenseFrom:999, drumBreakFrom:999, drumBreakTo:0 — i.e. breakdown and intensity staging are DISABLED. The 'fourfloor' drum branch doesn't even read intense/isBreak, so there is zero arrangement movement. No riser/build/crash/snare-roll code exists anywhere in the file.
gaps.fix: Needs the arrangement/automation engine (does not yet exist). Minimum viable: (1) give trance real drumBreakFrom/To (e.g. break bars 6-9: mute kick+bass, keep pad+lead, raise reverb) and re-entry. (2) Add a build-up generator at bars 8-9 in the drum scheduler: accelerating snare/clap roll (8th->16th->32nd subdivisions, ramp velocity) + a white-noise riser node automating up + filter-cutoff ramp + a crash on bar-10 step 1. (3) Mute/unmute whole busses via the existing duckGain/mute plumbing. This is the highest-leverage authenticity gain after the supersaw.

gaps.dnaWants: Closed hi-hats = a CONTINUOUS 16th-note stream (all 16 steps) with offbeat velocity accents; open hats on 3/7/11/15; ride doubling those in peak sections; tom/perc fills at phrase ends (steps 14-16).
gaps.currentImpl: fourfloor pushes ONE closed hat per beat (4 hats/bar, on the beat, vel 0.28) plus the offbeat open hat. So the hi-hat layer is 4 on-beat closed hats, not a 16th stream. No ride, no fills.
gaps.fix: In the 'fourfloor' branch, replace the single on-beat hat with a 16th loop: for (e=0;e<4;e++) push a closed hat at beatT + e*(secBeat/4) with velocity accenting the offbeats (e.g. e%2===1 -> 0.4, else 0.22). Keep the open hat on the +. Optionally add a ride lane on 3/7/11/15 gated to peak/drop bars, and a tom/hat fill on the last bar of each 4/8-bar phrase (steps 14-16).

gaps.dnaWants: Harmonic minor as the signature lead/melody scale (raised 7th -> the augmented-2nd b6->7 'trance interval'); sus2/sus4/add9 pad voicings; lead doubled at octave + 3rd/6th; classic loops like i-VI-III-VII or vi-IV-I-V.
gaps.currentImpl: Mode is 'aeolian' (natural minor) for everything incl. the lead, so the raised-7th trance interval never occurs. padProg = [[0,2,4],[3,5,7],[-2,0,2],[-1,1,3]] are plain diatonic triads (no sus/add9). No octave/3rd harmony layer on the lead — generateMelody returns a single monophonic line.
gaps.fix: Two parts: (a) For the lead/melody scale, use harmonic minor (raise the 7th degree) while keeping pads/bass in aeolian — pass a separate melodyScale to the lead generator so the b6->7 interval appears. (b) Add a harmony layer: post-process the lead line into a second voice at +octave and a diatonic 3rd (reuse degreeToMidi with degree+scaleLen and degree+2) and play both through the supersaw for the anthem 'wall'. (c) Optionally voice pads as sus2/add9 by using triads like [0,1,4] / [0,2,4,8] in padProg.

gaps.dnaWants: Lead frequently plays ARPEGGIATED 16th-note figures that subdivide the slow chords, in addition to sustained anthem phrases.
gaps.currentImpl: leadGenA/B = generateMelody (free motif melody, outrun profile), arp:'off'. No arpeggiated 16th figure following the chordMap; an arpExpand/generateArpLead helper exists but trance doesn't use it.
gaps.fix: For the groove/intro sections, add a background arp using the existing generateArpLead(scaleLen, chordMap, {mode:'updown', octaves:2, stepDur:1}) to get a 16th-note arp that subdivides each slow chord, and reserve the sustained generateMelody line for the drop/anthem. This gives the 'background arps' the DNA calls for in the first-climax section.

topFixes: Replace the lead's 'crystal' FM bell with a real SUPERSAW voice (7 detuned fatsawtooth, bright cutoff with slow filter-env open, chorus + ping-pong delay + reverb). This is the single biggest identity miss — trance without a supersaw anthem lead does not read as trance.

Build a dedicated trance rolling bass (BASS_PROFILES.trance): omit the on-kick 16ths (steps 1/5/9/13), play all offbeat 16ths at the chord root, octave-jump the 16th just before each beat. The current outrun groove does the inverse (octaves ON the beats) and never omits under the kick.

Add the breakdown -> build-up -> drop arrangement engine (riser + accelerating snare roll 8th->16th->32nd + filter open + crash, with bus mute/unmute for the beatless breakdown). Currently breakdown/intensity are hard-disabled (drumBreakFrom:999) and the form is a flat 16-bar loop — the genre's defining macro-dynamic is entirely absent.

Make the closed hats a continuous 16th stream with offbeat accents (4 hats/bar -> 16) and switch the lead scale to harmonic minor with an octave+3rd harmony layer for the 'trance interval' and anthem-wall sound.

matches: Half-time drum identity is correct: snare on beat 3 only (step 9), NOT 2&4 — the single most important half-time signifier is implemented in the 'halftime' branch (synthwave_surfer.html:1885)
Kick on step 1 plus a syncopated ghost kick on the '+' before the snare (beat 2.5, step ~7) — matches the canonical minimal kick-on-1-and-7 pattern (line 1883-1884)
Closed hats are offbeat-leaning with a deliberate GAP around beat 3 to give the snare space, exactly as the DNA describes (line 1886)
Bar-2 fill: a 1/32 ascending-velocity hat roll into the downbeat (line 1888-1890) — matches 'bar 2 adds a fill leading back to the downbeat'
Tempo at defaultBpm 140 and defaultMode 'phrygian' are spot-on for dark classic dubstep tonality

The wobble architecture exists in principle: bass LFO is tempo-locked (line 1780-1781) and routed to a resonant 24 dB/oct lowpass cutoff (rolloff -24, line 1454; lfoTarget 'filter' connects lfo→filter.frequency, line 1546-1551) on a fatsawtooth osc — the correct synced-LFO-to-cutoff skeleton
Snare gets a big gated-reverb tail (triggerGatedReverb, line 1900) — supports the 'dub'-heritage reverberant snare
Bass groove is a static droning root ([[8,8]], octaveProb 0, moveProb 0) which correctly honors 'most classic dubstep loops drone on one root' for the harmony axis

gaps.dnaWants: Per-bar AUTOMATED wobble rate — the LFO rate is performed between 1/4, 1/8, 1/2, 1/16 (plus triplet/dotted) across the bar to make the defining 'wub-wub-WUBWUBWUB' growl; per-bar rate automation is called non-negotiable and the single highest-value engine feature
gaps.currentImpl: wobble override sets ONE static rate for the whole track: lfo.frequency = (bpm/60)*wobbleRate = 2.33 Hz fixed (line 1780-1781), wobbleRate:1 hardcoded (line 1352). No per-bar or per-step rate change anywhere — the LFO just oscillates at a constant quarter-note rate forever
gaps.fix: Add a per-bar wobble-rate schedule. Define e.g. wobbleRates:[1,1,2,4] (cycles per beat → 1/4,1/4,1/8,1/16 feel) and in scheduleAll, for each bar push a Tone.Transport.schedule that ramps g.bassBus.lfo.frequency.value = (bpm/60)*wobbleRates[bar%4]. Quantize the LFO phase to the bar so each new rate restarts cleanly. This single change is the difference between 'a sub bassline' and 'dubstep'.

gaps.dnaWants: TWO co-existing layers: an UNMODULATED clean sine sub at 20-80 Hz carrying the root + a separate gritty modulated mid-bass growl from ~80 Hz up, EQ/crossover-split so they coexist
gaps.currentImpl: Single fatsawtooth MonoSynth (count:2, spread:10) does both jobs (alkali model, line 1452); the same osc that wobbles is also the only low end. No clean sine sub layer exists; lows wobble away with the cutoff, gutting the felt fundamental
gaps.fix: Add a parallel sine sub voice for the dubstep bass: a second Tone.MonoSynth (oscillator sine, fixed lowpass ~120 Hz, NO LFO) triggered from the same bassEvents one octave below, summed pre-master and NOT routed through the wobble LFO. Keep the fatsawtooth as the wobble/growl layer. Optionally high-pass the wobble layer ~80 Hz so the two don't clash. This is engineRequirement 'two-layer bass voice'.

gaps.dnaWants: The DROP is the defining dynamic event: intro → build/riser+filter-sweep+silence → DROP onto sub+wobble on a downbeat → breakdown → build → DROP 2. Heavy use of silence/empty bars; sparse, minimal arrangement
gaps.currentImpl: Uses buildOutrunForm — a flat continuous 16-bar loop with drums from bar 0 (drumStartBar:0) and no dynamics: drumIntenseFrom:999 and drumBreakFrom:999 mean intensity/break NEVER fire (line 1348). No intro, no riser, no build, no silence-then-downbeat resolution, no second drop. Everything plays at once for all 16 bars
gaps.fix: Author a buildDubstepForm with explicit sections on a 16-bar grid: bars 0-3 intro (pad only, no drums/bass), bar 4 = build (1/32 hat roll accelerando + filter-sweep-up on pad + snare-rush), bar 5 DROP (bass+wobble+drums enter on the downbeat after a beat of silence), bars 5-10 body, bars 11-12 breakdown (drums out, pad swell + reverb tail), bar 13 build, bar 14 DROP 2. Requires the build/riser/drop engine (see needsLayerC).

gaps.dnaWants: Bass riff is rhythmically SYNCOPATED, leaving holes in the half-time grid (off-grid stabs against the kick/snare), with optional tritone/Phrygian b2 dissonance baked into the riff
gaps.currentImpl: BASS_PROFILES.dubstep grooves are [[8,8]] — two straight half-note roots filling the whole bar with zero syncopation and zero rests (line 731). It drones but never leaves a hole or lands off-grid; no b2/tritone color in the riff
gaps.fix: Replace the groove with syncopated cells that leave gaps and align to the wobble, e.g. grooves:[[3,1,4,4,4],[4,2,2,4,'rest'-style],[6,2,8]] in 16th units, and allow a Phrygian b2 (degree shift to scale index 1) or tritone (degree to b5) on an accented step via a small dubstepMoveProb. Keep degree 0 on the downbeat for the drone, but put rests/holes on beats 2 and 4 so the wobble pulses through empty space.

gaps.dnaWants: Snare layered with a high-passed clap for the top; occasional open-hat accent on step 15 ('and' of beat 4); light shaker/perc on steps 2,6,10,14; ghost-note velocity variation on hats
gaps.currentImpl: Snare is a single bandpassed noise burst (no clap layer, line 1632-1635); halftime branch fires no openhat and no shaker/perc track; hats have only coarse velocity (0.22 / 0.12 ghost)
gaps.fix: In the 'halftime' branch: layer a high-passed clap on the beat-3 snare (trigger g.hat or a dedicated clap with HPF ~800 Hz alongside the snare at step 9); add an openhat accent at beatT(3)+secBeat/2 (step 15); add light perc/shaker hits at steps 2,6,10,14 (vel ~0.12); add subtle per-step velocity jitter on the offbeat hats for ghost-note feel.

gaps.dnaWants: Pads/harmony in the same dark mode as the rest (Phrygian/minor), holding the root; static drone or a slow i-VI-III-VII / i-bII move
gaps.currentImpl: Pad triggering hardcodes AEOLIAN for degreeToMidi (line 1832) while melody/bass use the selected phrygian melodyScale — so the pad's b2 color (the signature Phrygian menace) is lost and pads can sit a half-step off the bass's Phrygian degrees. padProg [[0,2,4],[0,2,4],[-1,1,3],[-1,1,3]] is a reasonable slow i↔VII move but in the wrong scale
gaps.fix: Pass melodyScale (or algo.padMode) into the pad event builder instead of the hardcoded AEOLIAN at line 1832 so the pad voices Phrygian triads consistent with the bass/lead. For maximum classic-dubstep menace, also offer a drone padProg of all [[0,2,4]] (static tonic) and add a i-bII option (root then b2 triad) to exploit the Phrygian half-step.

gaps.dnaWants: Either the bass riff doubled as the 'lead', OR a melodic supersaw/pluck carrying a dark minor melody with tritone/b2 intervals, big reverb + dotted-1/8 delay
gaps.currentImpl: leadGenA and leadGenB both return [] (line 1344-1345) — there is no lead voice at all. Defensible for ultra-minimal classic dubstep, but no melodic-dubstep option and no detuned-stab/pluck variant
gaps.fix: Add an optional sparse stab lead: a detuned saw pluck (fast filter-env, ~200 ms decay, unison detune, send:true for the dotted-1/8 delay that's already wired at line 1600) playing the root/b5 on the snare backbeat (beat 3) for a classic dubstep stab. Keep it optional/sparse so the empty space is preserved.

topFixes: Add per-bar AUTOMATED wobble-rate scheduling (1/4→1/8→1/16 across the bar) — currently a single static 2.33 Hz LFO; this is THE genre identity and the highest-value single change (the synced-LFO-to-cutoff plumbing already exists, only the per-bar rate automation is missing)

Split the bass into two layers: keep the fatsawtooth as the modulated wobble/growl, and ADD a parallel unmodulated sine sub one octave down (no LFO, fixed LP ~120 Hz) so the felt fundamental survives the cutoff sweep

Author a buildDubstepForm with a real DROP: intro (pad only) → riser/build with filter sweep + 1/32 hat rush + a beat of silence → bass+wobble+drums slam in on a downbeat → breakdown → build → DROP 2; replace the flat buildOutrunForm and the disabled drumIntenseFrom/drumBreakFrom (999)

Make the bass groove syncopated with holes (replace [[8,8]] with cells that rest on beats 2 & 4) and inject a Phrygian b2 / tritone accent so the wobble pulses through empty space instead of a continuous droned half-note

matches: Four-on-the-floor kick is correct: 'fourfloor' drum branch puts a kick on every beat (steps 1,5,9,13), matching the DNA canonical kick.
Clap/snare backbeat is on beats 2 and 4 (beat===1||beat===3), matching DNA CLAP 5,13.
Open hat on the offbeat is present: 'openhat' on beatT + secBeat/2 of every beat = the '+' of each beat, matching the DNA open-hat-on-offbeat signature gesture (DNA's offbeat-8ths 3,7,11,15).
Tempo (defaultBpm 124) sits inside the DNA range 118-130 and near typical 122.

Single-key static modal vamp: padProg is one chord [[0,2,4]] repeated, buildAcidForm loops one 16-step ostinato across all 16 bars with no progression — matches 'i (static one-chord modal vamp)' and 'minimal-to-no harmonic change'.
phrygian default mode is a legitimate DNA scale ('Phrygian / Phrygian-dominant for a darker Eastern acid flavour').

Bass note generator is root-centric with octave-up (+scaleLen) and octave-down (-scaleLen) jumps plus occasional b2/2 steps, matching DNA 'octave jumps (+12 on scattered steps)' and 'random modal/pentatonic notes with octave jumps genuinely work'.
Per-step slide and accent FLAGS exist on each of the 16 steps, and slide is wired to portamento (0.055s) in the scheduler — the single most important 303 gesture is at least partially modeled.
sidechain: true with master sidechain 0.5 gives the pumping house glue.
Bass voicing has high resonance (0.7 -> Q~12.6) and env-mod (fenvAmount 0.7) with a short fenvDecay (0.22) — a per-note resonant filter pluck, the core squelch ingredient.

gaps.dnaWants: The TB-303 squelch 'IS the genre' — a single-oscillator saw monosynth with a 3-pole resonant lowpass, env-mod pluck, portamento slide, accent-coupled cutoff, and POST-FILTER distortion that screams at the resonant peak. The HTML even defines a dedicated 'acid' model (single sawtooth, portamento 0.05, Q 10, Chebyshev waveshaper) for exactly this.
gaps.currentImpl: ENGINE_VOICINGS.acid.bass.model is 'alkali', NOT 'acid'. The purpose-built acid MonoSynth (single saw + Chebyshev) is defined in buildSynthModel but never wired into any voicing. Alkali is a 2-osc fatsawtooth (count:2 spread:10) through a generic Tone.Distortion at character 0.7*0.5=0.35. So the genre's defining instrument is substituted by a detuned-saw house bass.
gaps.fix: Change ENGINE_VOICINGS.acid.bass.model from 'alkali' to 'acid'. The 'acid' model is already correct (oscillator type 'sawtooth' single osc, portamento 0.05, filter Q 10 rolloff -24, filterEnvelope octaves 4, Chebyshev(4) post-filter waveshaper). Then bump its Chebyshev wet from 0.15 toward 0.3-0.4 and raise characterAmount so the resonant peak audibly distorts (DNA: 'distortion interacting with the resonant peak is essential').

gaps.dnaWants: ACCENT must boost BOTH amplitude AND filter cutoff via a separate accent envelope ('it is not just velocity') — accented notes pop brighter, not just louder.
gaps.currentImpl: In scheduleAll the accent path only changes triggerAttackRelease velocity (1.0 vs 0.65). There is no cutoff/filter-envelope boost on accented steps. This is the exact 'house but not acid' failure the DNA risk-note warns about ('a generic resonant filter without true accent-coupling sounds house but not acid').
gaps.fix: In the bass makePart callback, when v.accent is true, momentarily raise the filter envelope before triggering: e.g. g.bassModel.synth.filterEnvelope.octaves = base+1.5 and/or g.bassModel.synth.filterEnvelope.baseFrequency *= 1.6 for that note (restore on non-accented steps), in addition to the 1.0 velocity. Couple amp+cutoff so accents are brighter, not just louder.

gaps.dnaWants: Real-time filter movement (slowly opening/closing CUTOFF and riding RESONANCE over many bars) is the PRIMARY source of evolution and the main arrangement device — 'the filter opening up is the build'. Listed as a non-negotiable identityMustHave.
gaps.currentImpl: acid.bass.lfoTarget is 'off' and there is no automation lane. The bass filter cutoff is static for the whole 16 bars. The ONLY evolution mechanism (filter-sweep performance) is entirely absent on the bass. The static loop never morphs.
gaps.fix: Set acid.bass.lfoTarget:'filter' with a very slow lfoRate (~0.04-0.08 mapped Hz, i.e. a 20-40s sweep) and lfoDepth ~0.5 so the cutoff slowly opens/closes — the applyTrackParams 'filter' branch already sweeps around the cutoff slider. Better: add a multi-bar cutoff automation ramp tied to form position (cutoff rising across bars 0-8, peaking, easing) to emulate the performed filter open. This is the single highest-authenticity-per-effort change after switching the model.

gaps.dnaWants: Variable 303 pattern length INDEPENDENT of the 16-step drum loop (13/15/odd steps) so the line phases against the 4/4 drums, generating evolving variation from static material (explicitly called out in form + engineRequirements).
gaps.currentImpl: generateAcidLine produces exactly 16 steps and buildAcidForm repeats the identical riff every bar locked to the drum grid (riff.map per bar, same line). No phasing, no per-bar variation — every bar is byte-identical.
gaps.fix: Generate an odd-length step array (e.g. 15 steps) and index it modulo its length across a continuous 16-step-per-bar cursor so the 303 drifts against the drums (step = line[(globalStep) % 15]). This gives free evolving variation that, combined with the filter sweep, is what makes a static acid loop listenable for minutes.

gaps.dnaWants: Arrangement-by-subtraction/addition: intro builds drums element-by-element (kick, then hats, then clap) over 8-16 bars; drop the 303 in and let it run; breakdowns strip to bare drums or bare 303; long DJ-friendly intros/outros. The 'build' is the filter opening, not a synth riser/impact.
gaps.currentImpl: drumStartBar 0 / drumEndBar 16 with drumIntenseFrom 999 and drumBreakFrom 999 — i.e. NO build, NO break, NO subtraction. Full drum kit and full 303 from bar 0 to 16, flat. The sustained 'lead' pad only enters at bar 8 (the only arrangement event). No layered intro, no breakdown.
gaps.fix: Stage the drums: kick-only bars 0-3, add closed hats bar 4, add open-hat + clap bar 8 (gate hat/openhat/snare pushes on bar thresholds inside the fourfloor branch). Optionally strip to bare 303 for a 2-bar breakdown via drumBreakFrom/To. This is achievable in the existing engine WITHOUT a riser/drop FX engine — the 'build' is purely additive layering + the filter sweep.

gaps.dnaWants: Acid house usually has NO distinct lead — the 303 is lead, bass and hook simultaneously. If a secondary voice appears it is a SECOND 303 line an octave up with its own slides/accents, a filtered acid arp, or a percussive vocal stab — never a soft sustained pad-melody.
gaps.currentImpl: leadGenA uses generateSustainedLead (whole-bar held chord tones / rests) on a 'vapor' Juno-style chorused fatsaw pad with 0.5s attack, 2.0s release, lfoTarget filter. That is a soft atmospheric pad-lead, the opposite of a percussive 303/stab aesthetic. leadGenB returns [] (fine).
gaps.fix: Either drop the lead entirely (most authentic — set leadGenA to return []), or replace it with a second acid-model voice an octave up running generateAcidLine with its own slide/accent through the same resonant+distortion chain, entering only in a later section. Avoid the chorused vapor pad as a 'lead'.

gaps.dnaWants: Either no pad at all, or a cold low-passed string/organ wash used purely for breakdown atmosphere, sitting quietly behind the 303 with low-cut reverb above ~400Hz so it doesn't clutter the sub.
gaps.currentImpl: Pad is a vapor fatsaw on a single [0,2,4] triad, bars 8-16, volume 0.12 — quiet and minimal, which is acceptable. It is acceptable but the triad reinforces a 'chord' where the genre prefers root+fifth/octave drones or no harmony.
gaps.fix: Optional: reduce the pad chord to a power-interval drone (root+fifth or root+octave: padProg [[0,4]] or [[0,0]] with octave) rather than a full triad, and restrict it to a breakdown section only, to stay inside 'power-interval drones' / 'minimal-to-no harmonic change'.

gaps.dnaWants: Closed-hat 16ths (every step 1-16) for the propulsive shimmer in the busy variant, plus optional light MPC swing 54-58% on hats for the groovier 909/UK feel; open-hat choke by the next closed hat.
gaps.currentImpl: Closed hat fires only ON the beat (1 per beat = 4 per bar), not 16ths and not 8ths — sparser than either DNA option. swing:0 (acceptable for the raw Chicago variant). No open-hat choke modeling.
gaps.fix: Add closed hats on 8ths or 16ths (loop e=0..1 or e=0..3 within the beat like the 'shuffle' branch) for the propulsive shimmer; optionally set swing ~0.08-0.12 for the grooved 909 variant. Choke is minor given the 8n openhat decays quickly.

topFixes: Switch ENGINE_VOICINGS.acid.bass.model from 'alkali' to the already-built 'acid' MonoSynth (single saw + portamento + Q10 resonant LP + Chebyshev post-filter distortion) and raise its distortion wet — this restores the genre's single defining instrument with zero new code.

Add real-time bass filter automation: set bass.lfoTarget:'filter' with a slow rate + depth, or a multi-bar cutoff ramp tied to form position, so the static loop evolves via the performed filter sweep (a non-negotiable identityMustHave that is currently fully absent).

Make ACCENT open the filter cutoff (via filterEnvelope boost) in addition to velocity in the bass makePart callback, so accented steps pop BRIGHTER not just louder — fixing the exact 'house but not acid' failure the DNA flags.

Stage the arrangement additively (kick-only intro -> add hats -> add clap/open-hat -> 303) and give the 303 an odd step length (15) that phases against the 16-step drum grid, so the loop builds and evolves without needing any riser/drop FX engine.
